mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value < 0.05, or rolling Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or event study alpha is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is insignificant in OLS regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side contraction that outweighs demand resilience, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of observations, or regime-split IC shows no directional advantage.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumer spending capacity, supporting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 60-day forward returns show negative mean during payroll growth periods, or Rank IC remains below -0.02 over consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL month-over-month increase above 0.4% triggers inflation fears and anticipated central bank tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in > 70% of high-inflation trigger months, or event study shows no statistically significant negative drift.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP combined with falling unemployment indicates an overheating economy where demand outpaces labor supply, compressing profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-UNRATE interaction term fails to predict negative 20-day returns, or joint signal yields positive alpha in > 60% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 1.5% quarterly during a post-shock recovery phase signals rapid capacity utilization and earnings acceleration, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth > 1.5% in recovery regimes produces negative 60-day SPX returns in > 55% of cases, or regime-specific IC is statistically indistinguishable from zero.
